observed_outcome.main_success_signal: selected candidate produces only partial_progress trajectories, eliminating early failures and low_progress_survival in its own evaluation

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: no success_like trajectories; safe_landing_bonus never fires due to overly strict condition
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured safe_landing_bonus to provide partial credit for sub-conditions and added intermediate rewards for leg contact and stability near pad
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: candidate generates 50 partial_progress trajectories but still no success_like achieved; safe_landing_bonus remains insufficient to trigger final terminal condition

lesson: Local repair that provides intermediate rewards for progress toward terminal condition can eliminate early failures and produce consistent partial progress, but bridging the gap to success_like requires aligning reward with the exact terminal state rather than intermediate approximations; pure partial-progress dominance does not imply success readiness.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as it drives partial_progress
avoid treating partial_progress as success_like in preference construction
strengthen terminal condition reward with progressive relaxation or shaping to encourage the final landing step
consider using curriculum learning or gradual tightening of safe_landing condition to guide policy from partial_progress to success_like

archive_update.reason: Candidate selected for long training and added to elite archive; no rollback needed as conditions not met.

uncertainties: No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
Selection based on weak pairwise evidence, conclusions may not generalize to success behavior
True success may require a fundamentally different reward structure not yet explored
